title: To My Cousin, Anne Bodham, On Receiving From Her A Network Purse Made By Herself.
content:
My gentle Anne, whom heretofore,
    When I was young, and thou no more
    Than plaything for a nurse,
    I danced and fondled on my knee,
    A kitten both in size and glee,
    I thank thee for my purse.


    Gold pays the worth of all things here;
    But not of love;�that gem�s too dear
    For richest rogue�s to win it;
    I, therefore, as a proof of love,
    Esteem thy present far above
    The best things kept within it.
meaning:
William Cowper's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "To My Cousin, Anne Bodham, On Receiving From Her A Network Purse Made By Herself."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
